summary: iOS | Morpheus | Unable to track inCall status
scenario: iOS app fails to detect active call state on Voyager 4245 Office (Morpheus) devices due to unsupported deckardId, preventing call-aware alerts and blocking of DFU/ANC changes
description_clean: The iOS app cannot reliably detect when a user is in an active call when connected to Morpheus (Voyager 4245 Office) devices. This prevents the app from showing appropriate alerts and blocking DFU (firmware updates) or ANC/transparency mode changes during calls. The root cause is that Morpheus devices do not support deckardId 0x0E35. The fix is to use deckardId 0x0E22 instead, which correctly reports call state for these devices. The Android team has already adopted this approach.
steps: 1. Connect a Morpheus (Voyager 4245 Office) device to the iOS device via Bluetooth.
2. Open the Poly Studio Mobile app on the iOS device.
3. Verify the device is shown on the Home screen.
4. Initiate or receive an active phone call on the iOS device.
5. While in the active call, observe the Poly Studio Mobile app for any call-state-related alerts or UI changes.
6. Attempt to trigger a DFU (firmware update) from the app.
7. Attempt to change ANC or Transparency Mode settings from the app (e.g., Poly Studio Mobile → [Device Name] → Audio Settings → ANC / Transparency Mode).
expected: The app should detect the active call state, display appropriate call-related alerts, and block DFU firmware updates and ANC/Transparency Mode changes during the call.
actual: The app does not reliably detect the active call state. No call-related alerts are shown, and DFU firmware updates or ANC/Transparency Mode changes are not blocked during the call.
